SAPIANTA.meaning: constitutional governance substrate and product governance identity

AGOL.meaning: governed operational layer family within SAPIANTA

hierarchy: AGOL -> governed operational layer family within SAPIANTA
AiGOL -> semantic-direction governance identity within AGOL

role_boundary.AiGOL / AGOL: semantic direction governance, replay, lifecycle, transport

naming_hierarchy.constitutional_layer: Use SAPIANTA for constitutional architecture, invariants, enforcement, lineage, substrate, and product identity.
naming_hierarchy.governance_layer: Use AGOL for governed operational governance primitives; use AiGOL only for semantic direction governance.
naming_hierarchy.runtime_layer: Use AGOL_BRIDGE_RUNTIME_* for filesystem bridge runtime; use GOVERNED_*_RUNTIME_* for non-bridge governed runtime surfaces.
naming_hierarchy.observability_layer: Use GOVERNED_*_OBSERVABILITY_* for read-only inspection and SIDEPANEL only for Browser Companion UX surfaces.
naming_hierarchy.adr_numbering_rules: Replace ADR-00X with stable ADR-### identifiers before constitutional finalization.
naming_hierarchy.finalize_milestone_rules: Use FINALIZE_<MILESTONE_ID>_V<N> unless the milestone id already includes a version suffix.

ambiguities_remaining: Top-level governance ADRs still use ADR-00X placeholder numbering.
Historical artifacts mix AGOL, AiGOL, AIGOL, and SAPIANTA naming.
AGOL Bridge Transport Spec v1 remains Draft v1 while implementation finalization exists.
Some sidepanel evidence uses pre-finalization Browser Companion naming.

constitutional_layers.execution: Codex / workers through governed transport only

recommended_followup: Record alias mappings for AGOL, AiGOL, AIGOL, and SAPIANTA.
Decide whether AGOL Bridge Transport Spec v1 becomes canonical.
Define whether historical artifact renaming is prohibited, optional, or migration-manifest governed.
